item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really liked this one - many anomalous objects of a ghostly nature simply are ghostly whereas this one is a bit deeper and just moving enough. I didn't find it over dramatic at all. Studying a ghost mall with a terrified Childs voice in it for days, weeks or months and then having her communicate, beg you for help… I think my reaction would be similar. I don't comment much - good job.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is such a well-written entry. There's mystery, emotional attachment, and clearly SOMETHING anomalous happened there, but even the Foundation isn't sure what. Just that some poor little girl, of whatever species, got caught up in the middle of it. Guaranteed upvote from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's not perfect (for reasons already noted), but it ain't that bad.

+1 for Fire-breathing ghost dragons.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It definitely evokes a sadness when you get to the end. +1 Solidly written

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There isn't a lot of speculation because there aren't a lot of loose threads to tie it to.
All the essential plot elements are clear:
Mom and girl go to astral/psychic mall, lizards and fire happens, the higher plane disaster spills over and causes ghosts/echoes in the "real" world.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That was really cool, and yes, the last log is the best.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Neutral vote for now.

I just don't know what to think. I know that I am missing some important piece of backstory/trivia (these are dragons or something) and I'm surprised there's not more discussion on this. I keep coming back waiting for more people to share their thoughts, but nope, 5 comments only. Come on people, I can't think for myself!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Dialog may be a bit OTT, but this makes for a good mystery. The last log is really interesting, and very tragic, in light of everything else. The core of this is really good, though I would like to see more about whatever was with that door. Not neccessarily in this article, but in a tale or, better yet, another SCP. It seems you have a clear idea of what happened there, and i would like to see more of it, but i'm not sure how that could be done here without disrupting the essence.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
intangible saurian figures

unexplained injuries in the form of burns

Here there be dragons?
Obviously, the interview log proves otherwise, but I'd still like to believe that this was a little dragon family.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Dialogue for the kid is pretty bad, I think you used the word "mommy" enough times. The last log was definitely the best of the bunch, although it still wasn't super good. I did like some of the implications of what they said, but the delivery was too stiff for my tastes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this one; it's a rich vein of But What If… moments. Up'd.
